title: Economic growth
description: Practice what you know about economic growth in this exercise.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following statements best describes economic growth?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: A sustained increase in real GDP per capita over time
Any sustained increase in inflation over time
Any increase in nominal GDP per capita over time

assessment_items.question_content:
**Economic growth is best defined as**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a sustained increase in real GDP per capita over time.
any increase in nominal GDP per capita.

assessment_items.question_content:
Real GDP increased from $\$400$ million to $\$500$ million in Hamsterville. Real GDP only increased from $\$400$ million to $\$440$ million in Atlantis.

**Which of the following best describes why Atlantis may actually have experienced more economic growth than Hamsterville?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Atlantis’ population is growing more slowly than Hamsterville’s population
The price level in Hamsterville may be increasing faster than in Atlantis
The price level in Atlantis might be increasing faster than in Hamsterville
Hamsterville’s population is growing more slowly than Atlantis’ population
None of the above, both countries are growing at the same rate

assessment_items.question_content:
[[☃ image 1]]

**Which of the following is a public policy that would cause the change from $Y_{F1}$ to $Y_{F2}$ shown in the graph?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: A coordinate grid plots six labeled straight lines on a graph. The vertical axis is labeled P L, and the horizontal axis is labeled real G D P. Two vertical lines are labeled L R A S subscript 1 and L R A S subscript 2, with L R A S subscript 2 to the right of L R A S subscript 1. Two upward-sloping lines from the bottom left to the top right are labeled S R A S subscript 1 and S R A S subscript 2, with S R A S subscript 2 above S R A S subscript 1. Two downward-sloping lines are labeled A D subscript 1 and A D subscript 2, with A D subscript 2 to the right of A D subscript 1. The vertical line L R A S subscript 1 drops to point Y subscript f 1 on the horizontal axis. The vertical line L R A S subscript 2 drops to point Y subscript f 2 on the horizontal axis.

choices.content: A decrease in the supply of public saving
Increasing taxes on research and development spending

assessment_items.question_content:
The average years of education per person in Islandia increased by 3 years over the period 2010 - 2015.

**What determinant of productivity changed, and how would that impact Islandia’s long-run aggregate supply curve (LRAS)?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Human capital increased; LRAS shifts right
Labor increased; no impact on LRAS
Technology increased; no impact on LRAS

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is NOT a determinant of productivity?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

The AD-AS model of Snaximus changed as shown in this graph:

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which of the following illustrates what would also be occuring in Snaximus’ production possibilities curve?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: A coordinate grid plots six labeled straight lines on a graph. The vertical axis is labeled P L, and the horizontal axis is labeled real G D P. Two vertical lines are labeled L R A S subscript 1 and L R A S subscript 2, with L R A S subscript 2 to the right of L R A S subscript 1. Two upward-sloping lines from the bottom left to the top right are labeled S R A S subscript 1 and S R A S subscript 2, with S R A S subscript 2 above S R A S subscript 1. Two downward-sloping lines are labeled A D subscript 1 and A D subscript 2, with A D subscript 2 to the right of A D subscript 1. The vertical line L R A S subscript 1 drops to point Y subscript f 1 on the horizontal axis. The vertical line L R A S subscript 2 drops to point Y subscript f 2 on the horizontal axis.

choices.content: ![A coordinate grid plots two labeled curves on a graph. The vertical axis is labeled Consumption goods, and the horizontal axis is labeled Capital goods. assessment_items.question_content:
The Production possibilities curve (PPC) of the nation of Snaximus has changed as shown in this graph:

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2eb3ddd6bb186326eb4bc1e52bb36a3ae271032f)

**Based on the change shown in this graph, what other change must have also occurred?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The long-run aggregate supply (LRAS) curve shifted to the right
The short-run aggregate supply (SRAS) curve shifted to the right
The aggregate demand (AD) curve shifted to the right
The long-run Phillips curve (LRPC) shifted to the right
The short-run Phillips curve (SRPC) shifted to the right

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following describe two ways to show economic growth in a graphical model?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: A shift to the right of the long-run aggregate supply (LRAS) curve; a shift outward of the production possibilities curve (PPC)
A shift to the right of the long-run aggregate supply (LRAS) curve; a shift to the right of aggregate demand (AD)
An outward shift of the production possibilities curve (PPC); a shift to the right of the long-run Phillips curve (LRPC)
A shift to the right of the long-run Phillips curve (LRPC); a shift to the right of the short-run Phillips curve (SRPC)
A movement from the interior of a production possibilities curve (PPC) to the curve; a shift to the right of aggregate demand (AD)

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following measures is typically used to approximate the productivity of an individual unit of labor in a country?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Which of the following is typically used as a measure of average labor productivity?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Which of the following best describes what leads to an increase in labor productivity?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: An equal increase in capital and workers
An equal increase in technology and workers

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best describes what leads to an increase in labor productivity?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: An equal increase in capital and workers
An equal increase in technology and workers

assessment_items.question_content:
**If all factors of production besides labor are held constant, then what happens in an economy if output increases?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**The aggregate production function is used to describe which of the following relationships?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The relationship between output and inputs (labor, capital, and technology)
The relationship between unemployment and inflation in the long run
The tradeoff between the production of capital goods and consumption goods
The tradeoff between unemployment and inflation in the short run
The relationship between output supplied and the price level

assessment_items.question_content:
**If all factors of production besides labor are held constant, then what happens in an economy if output increases?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**According to the aggregate production function, which of the following could cause an increase in real GDP per capita?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Which of the following best describes the relationship captured by the aggregate production function?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Output per worker is positively related to capital per worker
Output per worker increases at an increasing rate as more capital per worker is added
Productivity is inversely related to output per worker
Productivity decreases as more capital per worker is added
Increases in real GDP per capita happens only if capital per worker increases
